steps.title: Identify the justification signal
steps.content: The “because” clause introduces the reason: two Osage women were foremost twentieth-century ballet dancers. This precedent justifies the choice of ballet as the medium.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content:
Suitable means appropriate or fitting — exactly the meaning the context requires when a reason is given for a choice.

The other options don't fit: “determined” refers to resolve, “arbitrary” contradicts the stated reason, and “unpredictable” ignores causality.

This matches choice A — A is correct.
